summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on five cases. Two variances (BZA 2025-016 and 017) were approved allowing creation of flag lots at 5830 Deerfield Road. A variance (BZA 2025-019) for the Goshen Public Library to install an electronic message board sign at seven feet height was approved pending ODOT approval. Cases BZA 2025-020 and 2025-021 regarding an agritourism/farmer's market operation at 1867 Kerbet Road were continued to the next meeting after the board entered deliberative session and determined additional questions needed to be addressed.
decisions: BZA 2025-016: Approved variance allowing creation of flag lots in R3 district at 5830 Deerfield Road
BZA 2025-017: Approved variance allowing creation of more than two flag lots plus interior lot at 5830 Deerfield Road
BZA 2025-019: Approved variance for Goshen Public Library electronic message board sign at seven feet height, pending ODOT approval
Motion to enter deliberative executive session approved
BZA 2025-020 and BZA 2025-021: Continued to next meeting (November 17, 2025)
action_items: Zoning staff to publish findings of fact for BZA 2025-016 and 017 and process lot split forms for Rob Robison
Scott Conover/Goshen Library to check with ODOT regarding sign approval on State Route 132
Zoning staff to compile additional questions regarding BZA 2025-020 and 021 and email them to the Walkers
Staff to discuss traffic and speed concerns on Kerbet Road with Chief Rose
Zoning staff to coordinate with library sign applicant after ODOT consultation
key_amounts: 39.97 square feet proposed sign area for library sign
32 square feet maximum sign area for lots under 5 acres
50 square feet maximum sign area for lots of 5 acres or more
16.64 square feet electronic message board portion of library sign
6,240 square feet proposed pole building for farmer's market store
800 square feet current allowed farmer's market space
25,000 square feet minimum lot size in R3 district
7.5 acres planned for applicant's forever home at Deerfield Road
200 feet recommended paved entrance distance from Kerbet Road right-of-way
5 feet maximum sign height per code (7 feet proposed and approved for library sign)
notable_topics: Flag lot variances for residential property subdivision at 5830 Deerfield Road
Electronic message board sign variance for Goshen Public Library
Agritourism vs commercial use determination for proposed farmer's market on Kerbet Road
Traffic safety and speeding concerns on Kerbet Road near ball fields
Drainage concerns for neighboring properties near proposed farmer's market
ODOT approval requirements for signage on State Route 132
Vendor markets and food trucks on residential-zoned agricultural property
attendees: Scott Conover (Sign Graphics and Design, applicant representative for library sign)
John Grooms (neighbor, 1871 Kerbet Road, neutral on farmer's market)
Unnamed resident of 1890 Kerbet Road (in support of farmer's market)
notes: The transcript has significant audio/transcription artifacts and repeated 'Board of Trustees Meeting' headers throughout, likely from automated captioning errors. The meeting is actually a Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) hearing, not a Board of Trustees meeting despite repeated headers. Cases 2025-020 and 2025-021 were continued because the board had additional questions about the agritourism designation vs commercial use, traffic impacts, drainage, and paving requirements. The board entered a deliberative executive session before deciding to continue those cases. Next BZA meeting scheduled for November 17, 2025 at 6:30 PM.
title: BZA 10-20-25
